framework.name: GNAP Constrained Delegation Model
framework.description: GNAP explicitly models delegation as a negotiated grant with time-bounded, revocable access tokens issued to client instances by an AS on behalf of an RO. The grant is the 'constrained envelope' — it carries rights, duration, and can be rotated or revoked. This is structurally the same primitive as VRP mandates and session keys.
framework.raw_quote: GNAP defines a mechanism for delegating authorization to a piece of software and conveying the results and artifacts of that delegation to the software.

source.title: RFC 9635 - Grant Negotiation and Authorization Protocol (GNAP)
